en: I came to my garden, my sister, my bride, I gathered my myrrh with my spice, I ate my honeycomb with my honey, I drank my wine with my milk. Eat, friends, drink, and be drunk with love!
zh: 我的妹妹，我的新婦啊！我進了我的園中； 我採了我的沒藥與香料； 我吃了我的蜂房與蜂蜜； 我喝了我的酒和奶。 朋友們！你們要開懷吃喝； 親愛的啊！你們要不醉無歸。

en: I slept, but my heart was awake. A sound! My beloved is knocking. "Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my perfect one, for my head is wet with dew, my locks with the drops of the night."
zh: 我身雖然睡臥，我心卻醒。 這是我的良人的聲音，他敲著門說： “我的妹妹，我的佳偶， 我的鴿子，我的完全人哪！求你給我開門； 因為我的頭滿了露水， 頭髮給夜露滴濕。”

en: I had put off my garment; how could I put it on? I had bathed my feet; how could I soil them?
zh: 我回答：“我脫了外衣， 怎能再穿上呢？ 我洗了腳， 怎能再弄髒呢？”

en: My beloved put his hand to the latch, and my heart was thrilled within me.
zh: 我的良人從門孔裡伸進手來， 我的心因他大為激動。

en: I arose to open to my beloved, and my hands dripped with myrrh, my fingers with liquid myrrh, on the handles of the bolt.
zh: 我起來，要給我的良人開門； 我的兩手滴下沒藥， 指頭滴下沒藥， 滴在門閂上。

en: I opened to my beloved, but my beloved had turned and gone. My soul failed me when he spoke. I sought him, but found him not; I called him, but he gave no answer.
zh: 我親手給我的良人開門， 我的良人卻已轉身走了； 我發現他走了，差點昏倒； 我到處找他，卻找不見， 我呼叫他，他卻不回答。

en: The watchmen found me as they went about in the city; they beat me, they bruised me, they took away my veil, those watchmen of the walls.
zh: 城中的守衛巡邏的時候，找著我； 他們打了我，傷了我； 看守城牆的人奪去了我身上的外衣。

en: I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if you find my beloved, that you tell him I am sick with love.
zh: 耶路撒冷的眾女子啊！我囑咐你們： 你們若遇見我的良人， 你們要告訴他甚麼呢？ 你們要告訴他，我患了相思病。

en: What is your beloved more than another beloved, O most beautiful among women? What is your beloved more than another beloved, that you thus adjure us?
zh: 你這女子中最美麗的啊！ 你的良人比別人的良人有甚麼更可愛之處呢？ 你的良人比別人的良人有甚麼更可愛之處呢？ 以致你這樣囑咐我們呢？

en: My beloved is radiant and ruddy, distinguished among ten thousand.
zh: 我的良人紅光滿面， 是萬人中的表表者。

en: His head is the finest gold; his locks are wavy, black as a raven.
zh: 他的頭像精金， 他的頭髮如棕樹枝厚密， 像烏鴉那麼黑。

en: His eyes are like doves beside streams of water, bathed in milk, sitting beside a full pool.
zh: 他的眼好像溪水旁的鴿子， 在奶中洗淨，塑得合式。

en: His cheeks are like beds of spices, mounds of sweet-smelling herbs. His lips are lilies, dripping liquid myrrh.
zh: 他的兩頰如香花畦， 又如香草臺； 他的嘴唇像百合花， 滴下沒藥汁。

en: His arms are rods of gold, set with jewels. His body is polished ivory, bedecked with sapphires.
zh: 他的雙手像金管， 鑲嵌著水蒼玉； 他的軀體如象牙塊， 周圍包著藍寶石。

en: His legs are alabaster columns, set on bases of gold. His appearance is like Lebanon, choice as the cedars.
zh: 他的雙腿像大理石柱， 安放在精金的座上； 他的容貌如黎巴嫩山， 像佳美的香柏樹。

en: His mouth is most sweet, and he is altogether desirable. This is my beloved and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.
zh: 他的口甜蜜； 他全然可愛。 耶路撒冷的眾女子啊！ 這就是我的良人，我的朋友。
